master_row.title: Topological Helicity Quantisation
master_row.canonical_statement: Magnetic helicity takes discrete values proportional to the winding number defined around the TZP.
master_row.formation_inputs: Φ, V, A, B, x, w
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an integral expression relating Φ, V, A, B, x, w.
master_row.dictionary: The magnetic helicity (H=_V A B,d^3x) is quantised in units of the Trawinistic winding number, i.e., (H = w,), where () is the magnetic flux through the lemniscatic loop.
master_row.encyclopedia: Magnetic helicity becomes quantized when the field topology is constrained by integer TZP winding. Interpretive role: Quantized helicity is the doorway from field energetics into topology. Once helicity resolves into winding sectors, scale-invariant helicity ratios and linking-number laws can be stated without ambiguity about what counts as the conserved quantity.
